articles.title: Republicans push to end Homeland Security shutdown as shutdown fight shifts to reconciliation
articles.summary: After weeks of stalemate, GOP leaders are trying to use the budget process to force a final Homeland Security deal without Democratic votes — a high-stakes move that could shape the rest of Trump’s agenda.

articles.title: IMF warns war shock is slowing the world economy just as inflation flares anew
articles.summary: The Fund’s April outlook says conflict-driven energy costs are complicating central banks’ inflation fight, with new U.S. data showing prices accelerating again in March.

articles.title: NASA’s Artemis II mission turns the Moon into a laboratory for deep-space travel
articles.summary: The first crewed lunar flyby in more than 50 years is giving scientists a rare chance to study radiation, navigation, human performance and the Moon itself under real mission conditions.

articles.title: Cannes Leans Hard Into Auteur Cinema as Hollywood Pulls Back
articles.summary: The 2026 Cannes lineup is thinner on studio spectacle and heavier on prestige auteurs, underscoring a Hollywood that is still cautious about risk even after an encouraging box-office start to the year.

articles.title: OpenAI and Anthropic double down on AI safety as pressure shifts from model size to real-world risk
articles.summary: A wave of April announcements from two of the industry’s biggest labs shows how quickly the AI conversation is moving toward safety, security and public accountability.

articles.title: Microsoft’s Texas AI data-center pivot shows how fast the industry’s buildout is changing
articles.summary: OpenAI stepped back from expanding a massive Abilene campus, and Microsoft moved in — a sign that the AI infrastructure race is entering a more complex, more competitive phase.

articles.title: Nostalgia, tiny treats and tea rituals: why niche comfort culture is winning spring 2026
articles.summary: From 2016 throwbacks to miniature desserts and hojicha lattes, a cluster of very specific comforts is shaping what people are sharing, buying and eating right now.

articles.title: China softens tone on Taiwan after opposition leader’s Beijing visit, but the bigger contest remains unresolved
articles.summary: Xi Jinping’s meeting with Taiwan’s opposition leader produced a brief thaw in cross-strait rhetoric, yet the island’s security debate, defense spending fight and looming U.S.-China diplomacy keep the stakes high.

articles.title: Madonna’s ‘Confessions on a Dance Floor: Part II’ turns a pop-era classic into a 2026 comeback story
articles.summary: The queen of reinvention is returning to the sound that once reset her career, with a sequel album due July 3 and plenty of questions about what a 21st-century dance-floor confession sounds like.

articles.title: Sudan war enters fourth year as aid pledges rise, but fighting keeps the crisis out of reach
articles.summary: As Sudan marks three years since war exploded in Khartoum, diplomats are pledging more money and stronger coordination. But displacement, hunger and front-line violence continue to overwhelm relief efforts.
